segments.title: What is photosynthesis?
segments.content: Photosynthesis is how plants make food.

segments.title: Plants need sunlight
segments.content: Plants use sunlight to create energy.

segments.title: OVERLOAD: All concepts at once: intuition
segments.content: Start with the big idea: chlorophyll, photons, ATP. Focus on why it matters before adding detail.

segments.title: OVERLOAD: All concepts at once: core idea
segments.content: Now add the core detail: carbon fixation, glucose, light reactions. Chlorophyll captures photons, water splits, oxygen leaves, ATP carries energy, carbon fixation uses carbon dioxide, glucose stores energy, light reactions happen in thylakoids, dark reactions in stroma, photosystem I and II, electron transport chains, and NADPH.

segments.title: The equation
segments.content: The chemical equation is 6CO2 + 6H2O + light → C6H12O6 + 6O2.

segments.title: Analogy
segments.content: Think of photosynthesis like a solar panel: it converts light energy into stored energy.
